regions.text: Measuring how fast something is moving gives us the rate of motion. This plane has gone 300 miles in half an hour. Its rate was 600 m.p.h.
This man has walked two miles in half an hour. He was walking at a rate of four miles an hour. Now he is standing. He is not moving.

This man is working slowly. His pay will be small if he is paid by the amount of work he does in a day. This other man is working quickly. His pay will be high if he is paid by the amount of work done in a day.

The two commonest ways of paying a worker are by the hour and by the amount of work done (piecework). piece: bit. m.p.h.: miles per hour. pay: money for work. FUTURE: will pay. PRESENT: pay(s). PAST: paid.
